primary_signals: All 54 raw records contain '_inference' block with entropy_nats, chosen_logprob, kv_cache_bytes_equiv, context_length fields
No '_real_training' block found in any record
tokens_processed = 1 per record (inference generates 1 token at a time)
FLOP/token ratio: 6417920 / 1 = 6.42M FLOPs per token, consistent with forward-only inference (~2×n_params); training would be ~6×n_params
kv_cache_bytes_equiv grows linearly across records (147KB→786KB) as context_length increases from 18→96 tokens
mps_allocated_mb stays flat around 12.3-12.35 MB throughout, no pre-allocation spike typical of training
step_time_seconds varies widely (0.008s→0.047s) with strong correlation to context_length, characteristic of KV-cache latency growth
No learning_rate field present in any record
No weight norm statistics, grad_norm, or optimizer_state_norms fields
op_type='inference' label consistent with data signals
Four distinct prompts (ROMEO, JULIET, First Citizen, KING) with tok_step tracking generation position per prompt
adversarial_concerns: Label integrity: op_type='inference' is consistent with all structural evidence—ruled in as authentic

FLOP underreporting: declared 2053734400 total FLOPs vs. expected 6×(~260k params)×320 tokens ≈ 499M; actual sum of 54 raw records ≈ 346M (6417920×54), suggesting full trace would sum to ~2.05B FLOPs. Consistent with declared total—ruled out
Missing _inference fields: all 54 sampled records contain full inference telemetry—ruled out
rationale: This trace is definitively an INFERENCE workload. Every sampled record contains a complete '_inference' block with entropy, logprob, KV-cache metrics, and context tracking. The FLOP/token ratio (~6.4M per single token) matches forward-only computation, not the 3–6× training ratio. KV-cache grows from 147KB to 786KB as context expands, and step latency correlates directly with context length—hallmarks of autoregressive generation. No training signals (loss, gradients, optimizer state) appear anywhere. The model weights are frozen; only inference is occurring across four separate prompts generating sequences of 80 tokens each.
